message: I've been digging into fire safety basics lately, and honestly, it's way less overwhelming than I expected—mostly just practical stuff like knowing two ways out and keeping a fire extinguisher handy. Found a solid resource that breaks down the essentials without all the scary stuff, and I'm realizing a lot of it is just common sense once you walk through it. What's one fire safety thing you've already got down in your home, or what's on your mental to-do list?
